what_it_does: Records a two-phase build plan for a forked chat/messaging interface: a minimal single-member (tim) path that registers the app as a member with an HTTP receiver and SSE browser delivery, followed by a generalized multi-member, multi-project system with member picker, role resolution, project/channel switching, presence, and offline delivery.
observations: The plan is split into MINIMAL (one member) and THEN (generalize) sections.
The minimal path is explicitly framed as getting Tim out of the CLI.
The app registers as a named member with a port and HTTP receiver that accepts pushed injects and hands them to SSE.
Outbound messages use cc_channels.send to the build-fork's handle, with frm=tim and a thread id.
Fork replies route back over the same channel to tim, then to the app receiver, SSE, and browser, with a sent/seen indicator.
The fork's handle is captured on launch to avoid ambiguous self-resolution.
Generalized features include member picker, role resolution for the lead, project/channel switcher, member presence, and offline delivery.
Projects are equated to channels, each containing content, people, and conversation.
Existing fabric primitives (cc_channels.send/push/route_reply/live_sessions and named-channel registry) are marked as already built and verified.
New pieces are app-as-member registration/receiver, SSE to browser, member picker, and project switcher.
standouts: Strong reuse, don't rebuild directive that inventories existing fabric primitives versus new components.
App-as-member architecture: the browser-facing app is a named peer with its own receiver and SSE bridge.
Two-phase delivery strategy: single-user escape hatch before full multi-project chat.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item YAML frontmatter schema with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, created/updated timestamps, and history.
Uses board:// URI addressing and a part_of link to item-90a639e7.
Body uses Markdown headings and numbered lists for a build plan.
Refers to internal system identifiers (cc_channels, SSE, fabric) without defining them in the file.
concerns: Hard-codes a specific user name (tim) in the minimal path, which may complicate generalization.
Assumes the fork handle can be captured on launch but does not specify the mechanism.
States that offline delivery is already handled by the fabric, an assumption not verifiable from this file alone.
Blends low-level implementation details (ports, HTTP receiver, SSE) with product-level sequencing.
notes: A phased build plan that first wires a single user through an app-as-member receiver and SSE loop, then expands to a full multi-member, multi-project chat system.
questions: What is item-90a639e7 and how does this plan relate to it?
How does the app receiver authenticate or bind to a specific member and port?
What is the exact relationship between the fork, the app, and the browser client?
How is the fork handle captured and propagated on launch?
What does role resolution for the lead involve?
purpose_vs_actual: The title frames the file as a minimal first step plus generalization, and the body delivers exactly that build order, so purpose and content align.
apparent_themes: Incremental delivery via a minimal viable slice.
Reuse existing verified infrastructure rather than rebuild it.
Projects-as-channels model for organizing content, people, and conversation.
Presence, offline delivery, and read-receipt semantics.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to sequence implementation so the team can validate the app-as-member chat loop with one user before investing in the full multi-member, multi-project UI and role system.
novelty: It treats the browser-facing application as a first-class named member of the channel fabric with its own HTTP receiver and SSE bridge, rather than as a passive client or separate transport layer.
contribution: A concrete two-phase rollout plan, the app-as-member registration/receiver/SSE pattern, an explicit inventory of reusable fabric primitives versus new UI/UX pieces, and the projects-as-channels conceptual mapping.
summary_for_embedding: This noticeboard item is a phased build plan for a forked chat interface: first a minimal single-member app-as-member registration and SSE delivery loop for tim, then generalization to multi-member, multi-project chat with picker, role resolution, presence, and offline delivery.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to sequence implementation by reusing existing channel fabric to get one user out of the CLI before building the full member/project switcher system.
